raw_consumer_statement: I was contacted by credit card company on XXXX  and told there was possible fraud on my account. They reviewed the recent transactions and I was able to immediately confirm I did not make or authorize the purchases on the card. I told them I wanted to open a fraud complaint about the charges and they told me that unless I sent them a copy of my social security card, a copy of my passport, copy of my birth certificate, or a copy of my drivers license, they could not open a fraud complaint. They also indicated they were contacted in XXXX of XXXX  by someone wanting to change the telephone contact number associated with the account. I did not contact or authorize and changes to my account. I was able to login to my online account and observed fraudulent charges beginning in XXXX of XXXX  to XXXX and XXXX. The last statement balance on line is {$2600.00}. The email address the company has to send the statements to was hacked earlier this year. I no longer have access to receive emails on that account and have began migrating emails to XXXX. I did not notify this company of the email issue because I had a {$0.00} balance and was not going to use the card in the future. Also I had many other cards and accounts to change information and passwords due to whatever data leak with my information was causing me at that time.
